name: Q4019880
abstract: Will Mastin (June 20, 1878 – March 14, 1979), also credited as Will Maston, was a dancer and singer.

concerts.label: Benefit: Al Jolson Nation-Wide Servicemen's Free Canteen

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: And the winter of '36, I went up to join her, and that's when I met the Will Mastin Trio.

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Was this like the T. O. B. A. [Theater Owners' Booking Association]? Like Sammy Davis, Will Mastin, the kind of things they were doing? [INTERVIEWEE] Definitely.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: I arranged for him and his father when they first come into the Apollo Theatre. >> [INTERVIEWER] Was that the Will Mastin Trio. [INTERVIEWEE] Will Mastin Trio, yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: I remember him being with Will Mastin and his dad and his uncle, the Will Mastin trio. I remember us hanging out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Was this with the Will Mastin trio? [INTERVIEWEE] Yes, the Will Mastin trio. That’s right.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: And Will Mastin, who is the uncle of Sammy, the Will Mastin Trio?
